generator: Phase 1 AI read of STEP-1-INVARIANTS + engine-contracts/event-management-contracts.ts + 4 service files

freedomKeys.name: flow03_max_events_per_organizer
freedomKeys.currentDefault: per contracts (value TBD — read at P2)
freedomKeys.semantics: Max events an organizer can create per rolling calendar month before rate-limit error
freedomKeys.observableVia: POST /events response — RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED when threshold crossed; acme override lowers threshold (e.g. 50 → 3) to prove divergence

freedomKeys.name: flow03_max_attendees
freedomKeys.semantics: Default max attendees per event when organizer does not explicitly set capacity
freedomKeys.observableVia: GET /events/{id} — capacity field; registration returns WAITLIST when full; acme override (e.g. 100 → 10) proves divergence

freedomKeys.name: flow03_waitlist_max_size
freedomKeys.currentDefault: per contracts (contracts — verify at P2 read site)
freedomKeys.semantics: Max waitlist size before WAITLIST_FULL rejection when event at capacity
readBy: EventRegistrationManager (event-registration.service.ts — verify read site at P2)
freedomKeys.observableVia: Registration response — WAITLIST_FULL vs WAITLIST; acme override (e.g. 500 → 5) proves rapid waitlist saturation

freedomKeys.name: flow03_promotion_channels
freedomKeys.semantics: Comma- or array-encoded list of enabled promotion channels (in-app, push, email, sms)
freedomKeys.observableVia: NotificationsSent event payload — channels array; acme override (e.g. ['in-app','push','email'] → ['in-app']) proves channel pruning

freedomKeys.name: flow03_analytics_counter_ttl
freedomKeys.semantics: TTL (seconds) for analytics counter records before aggregation rollup
freedomKeys.observableVia: NOT DIRECTLY TENANT-VISIBLE — internal data retention; acme would rarely override profile-3 keys; include only for completeness

freedomKeys.name: flow03_campaign_engagement_threshold
freedomKeys.semantics: Minimum engagement score for PromotionCampaignCompleted to flag campaign as SUCCESSFUL
freedomKeys.observableVia: PromotionCampaignCompleted event payload — campaign.status SUCCESS vs BELOW_THRESHOLD; acme override (e.g. 0.6 → 0.3) proves faster-SUCCESS classification

chosenAdaptationForThisSession.rationale: Parallel to FLOW-02 successful TIER-D precedent. TYPE-A is the fastest demonstrable proof with tenant-observable behavior. Covers Req-3 (AI adaptation) without introducing grammar changes or role scope expansion.
proposedKeys: flow03_max_events_per_organizer  (organiser-facing rate limit — high UX visibility)
flow03_max_attendees  (capacity cap — high UX visibility in registration flow)
flow03_waitlist_max_size  (waitlist behavior divergence — edge case TYPE-D covered by same test)
flow03_promotion_channels  (notification fan-out divergence — touches FLOW-03→FLOW-04 boundary)
chosenAdaptationForThisSession.whyFourKeys: FLOW-02 proved 6 keys; FLOW-03 has 6 keys in surface but 4 are high-UX-visibility. Choosing 4 keeps Layer 1 spec focused while proving each orthogonal surface (creation gate, registration gate, waitlist gate, promotion fan-out).
excludedKeys: flow03_campaign_engagement_threshold (profile 2 but deep-internal state; deferrable to v1.0.x)

crossFlowBoundary.coInstallImpact: Installing FLOW-03 alone is valid for organiser experience (create + list events); installing without FLOW-04 degrades attendee experience (no RSVP, no waitlist, no check-in). A tenant consuming the full event lifecycle SHOULD install FLOW-03 + FLOW-04 together; tenant consuming organiser-only tooling CAN install FLOW-03 alone.

phase1Verdict: PASS — 6 FREEDOM keys with four-field catalog populated; at least 4 tenant-observable and scoped for Phase 2 adaptation. GAP-09 is NOT applicable (flow03_ prefix is consistent). READY for Phase 2 apply-and-verify.
